$comment: Layer C for BATCHSUM vs the real GnuCOBOL harness image: the record protocol's first symbolic outing. Record slot k is input variable k (domain from WS-AMT, the record's numeric twin); the READ inside the PERFORM UNTIL loop forks the record count through the unroller - the NOT AT END arm binds slot k, the AT END arm fixes the case at k records - so paths enumerate files of 0..max records and every realized case emits exactly that many stdin lines.

legacy.label: COBOL 85 BATCHSUM (GnuCOBOL via harness image legacymind/legacy-batchsum, record-stream wrapper)
